relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul is prisoner of Messiah on behalf of the nations.
Grace has a stewardship entrusted for them.
The mystery came by unveiling.
Reading lets them perceive his insight into Messiah’s mystery.
What was hidden in other generations is now unveiled in Spirit.
The nations are co-heirs, co-body, and co-sharers of promise in Messiah.
Paul serves this good news by grace and Divine power.
The least receives grace to announce unsearchable riches.
The hidden mystery is illuminated for all.
Through the assembly, many-sided Divine wisdom is shown to heavenly powers.
This unfolds according to the age-purpose made in Messiah.
In him there is bold access through his faithfulness/trust.
Paul’s pressure for them is not shame but glory.
He bends his knees toward the Father.
Every family in heaven and earth receives name from that Source.
The inner human is strengthened by Spirit-power.
Messiah dwells in hearts through trust, rooted and founded in love.
Together with all holy ones, they comprehend breadth, length, height, and depth.
Messiah’s love surpasses knowledge and fills toward all Divine fullness.
The Divine can do beyond what we ask or perceive by power working in us.
Glory fills the assembly and Messiah through all generations.

contemplative_note: Ephesians 3 is the mystery unveiled: nations as co-heirs and co-body, the assembly as many-sided wisdom display, and the heart rooted in love until it opens into Divine fullness.
